title: Got it — "Not Haro" is harasho's tag alias, so Monday counts as harasho's night. Let me rebuild this against their actual baseline.

turns.agentText:
Got it — "Not Haro" is harasho's tag alias, so Monday counts as harasho's night. Let me rebuild this against their actual baseline.

harasho tags under several aliases — "Not Haro", "Charrmander Harasho", "Haro Forge". Pulling their recent nights to trend Monday against.

Corrected report — with harasho's full recent history, **Monday was harasho's strongest night in a month**, not just a good isolated raid.

Here's the reframe: across 8 recent nights (May 25 – Jul 6), Monday's 1.63 K/D and 71% win rate are the best harasho has posted since June 6, coming off a rough patch (Jun 19–20, Jul 3 all below 1.0). Note harasho tags as a Troubadour support — personal damage (~250K) is expected to sit near the bottom, so this review judges squad outcomes under the tag, not their personal line.

K/D per night makes the arc clear — note the two deep dips were both heavy-outnumbered nights:

{{figure}}

(The May 25 spike — an 11-0 blowout at 11.8 K/D — flattens the rest of the scale; it was a farm night against an outmatched enemy.)

The full night-by-night, with fight count, record, and squad health:

| Date | Tag alias | Fights | W–L | Squad K/D | Squad deaths | Squad downs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 05-25 | Not Haro | 11 | 11–0 | 11.8 | 32 | 114 |
| 05-29 | Charrmander | 16 | 8–8 | 1.04 | 411 | 720 |
| 05-30 | Not Haro | 12 | 1–11 | 0.45 | 370 | 629 |
| 06-06 | Not Haro | 18 | 11–7 | 1.58 | 306 | 514 |
| 06-19 | Charrmander | 15 | 3–12 | 0.29 | 435 | 648 |
| 06-20 | Not Haro | 21 | 12–9 | 0.75 | 340 | 551 |
| 07-03 | Charrmander | 23 | 12–11 | 0.90 | 439 | 745 |
| **07-06** | **Not Haro** | **45** | **32–13** | **1.63** | **296** | **575** |

**Strengths**
- **Monday was a clear high point** — 1.63 K/D and 71% wins over a marathon 45 fights, out-downing the enemy 868 to 575 despite being outnumbered (avg 37 vs 32). Best result since June 6.
- **Kept the squad alive under a heavy load** — 296 squad deaths across 45 fights is the *lowest death-per-fight rate* (6.6) of any full night in the set except the May 25 farm; on Jul 3 it was 19 deaths/fight over 23 fights.
- **Willing to fight outnumbered every night** — enemy count exceeded squad size in all 8 raids, so the sub-1.0 nights aren't passivity; they're aggression against bad odds.

**Coaching points**
- **Know when the odds are unwinnable.** The two worst nights — May 30 (39 vs **73**, 1–11) and Jun 19 (44 vs 51, 3–12) — were bleed-outs. Those 800+ combined squad deaths tanked the month's K/D. Calling a disengage/objective-flip instead of repeated pushes into a doubled enemy would protect the record and morale.
- **Tighten the down-to-death conversion.** Monday still finished ~51% of its own downs into deaths (296/575). When you're winning the down war 868–575, more of those should rally — worth a word to the backline about peeling/cleave-rezzing after a push.
- **Monday's squad was smaller (32 vs the usual 40–46).** It worked — but the fights were also longer/more numerous. Worth watching whether the smaller, tighter group is *why* the K/D was better, and leaning into that rather than always stacking bodies.

**Double down on:** the Monday formula — a tighter squad, picking fights you can out-cleave and out-strip, and grinding a long session. It produced your best night in a month. **Change:** the hero-call into hopeless odds — recognize the 2-to-1 nights early (like May 30) and pivot to objectives instead of feeding the K/D.

One note: I tried to save "Not Haro = harasho" to durable memory but the memory tool hit an internal error, so it didn't stick — I'll re-attempt next time, or you can tell me to retry. Want me to line this up against the other active tags (Mesmer Kamoidra, Prez Ashton) for a commander-vs-commander comparison?

data.title: harasho — squad K/D per night (May 25 – Jul 6)
